findings.issue: Whole dashboard is hidden behind a mandatory 'Logg på med Apple' wall (new since the apple-signin-button merge). Sign-in fails with 'Kunne ikke starte Apple-innlogging (AUTHENTICATION_FAILED)' and there is no fallback/skip — no agenda, header masthead, events, or 'Dette dekker vi' section render at all. The product's core promise (a calm scannable agenda) is completely invisible.
Same as 375px: only the Apple sign-in card and AUTHENTICATION_FAILED error are visible; no dashboard content renders.
Same as smaller widths: only the Apple sign-in card and AUTHENTICATION_FAILED error are visible; no dashboard content renders.

notes: Could not assess ANY of the correctness/layout checklist (match name truncation, streaming channel/foreign-broadcaster check, times, flags/crests, must-see accent, header/footer) at any width — everything behind the auth gate is unreachable without a successful Apple sign-in. Yesterday's run (2026-07-20, before this gate landed) passed cleanly with full dashboard content visible.
The gate screen itself (wordmark, lead copy, 'Logg på med Apple' button, error text) is well laid out at all three widths: no overflow, no clipping, no truncation, calm and centered.

index.html comments this as a deliberate owner decision ('eier-beslutning 21.07'): body.gated hides all content until CloudKit sign-in succeeds. The AUTHENTICATION_FAILED error comes from icloud-sync.js's CloudKit setUpAuth() rejecting — plausibly because this screenshot harness (self-served docs/, no network per this agent's own instructions) cannot reach Apple's CloudKit web services, so this may be an artifact of the offline screenshot environment rather than a bug real signed-in users hit. Flagging as high anyway because it means visual QA (and any anonymous/non-authenticated visitor) can no longer see the dashboard at all — a human should confirm whether real users reliably complete Sign in with Apple in production, and whether an anonymous/public view is still intended given the product's calm-dashboard vision.
